title: Stellina
intro: Stellina, also known as Stellaria media, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the carnation family. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy in traditional herbal medicine. Stellina contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin issues.
taxonomy: Stellina is a genus of flowering plants in the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as bellflowers. This family is a part of the larger angiosperm clade and is primarily composed of annual and perennial herbaceous plants. The Campanulaceae family is divided into several subfamilies, with Stellina belonging to the subfamily Lobelioideae. The classification of Stellina is further refined at the species level, with several species being recognized within the genus. The taxonomy of Stellina continues to be refined as new research emerges.
